text: Ran the lexintel plugin's multi-step legal-memo pipeline for the HOTEL workspace, stepping through the A-section research stages up to A20 and then pushing the whole thing end-to-end. Diagnosed failures at steps A8, A10, and A19, all traced to auto-recompiles: the compiler regenerates each tool's @beta_tool wrapper using a naming convention (wrapper name == module name), but govuk_enforcement.py broke the convention by naming its wrapper enforcement_search, so the regenerated A8-evidence.py and A10-enforcement.py called the wrong function. Chose the fastest path to a full run via a --stale pass, which reran only the 27 dirty/upstream-stale steps in dependency order (A12-A18, A20, the sixteen B-section drafts, then C1 QA, C2, and C3 reassembly) while skipping the 12 already-fresh expensive agent steps. The background run finished clean in about 17 minutes, leaving 39/39 steps fresh and memo.md fully regenerated at pipeline/workspaces/HOTEL/memo.md; the only remaining issue is a cosmetic validate.py warning about A20's gate output being produced but never consumed.
